channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 in AI and Climate-Responsive Urban Design at University of Groningen
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 at the University of Groningen offers a unique and forward-thinking research position for individuals passionate about youth participation, climate-responsive cities, and the ethical integration of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in urban design. Hosted by the Faculty of Spatial Sciences, this fully funded PhD project explores how AI can transform […]

The post Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 in AI and Climate-Responsive Urban Design at University of Groningen appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 at the University of Groningen offers a unique and forward-thinking research position for individuals passionate about youth participation, climate-responsive cities, and the ethical integration of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in urban design. Hosted by the Faculty of Spatial Sciences, this fully funded PhD project explores how AI can transform participatory urban planning through inclusive and interdisciplinary approaches.

With applications open until 30 April 2026 (23:59 Dutch local time), this opportunity is ideal for candidates seeking to contribute to cutting-edge research that bridges technology, society, and sustainable urban development.

Innovative Research Focus on AI and Participatory Urban Design

This PhD project centers on a groundbreaking concept known as “Vibe-Designing”, a co-design methodology that integrates Generative AI into participatory urban design processes. The approach positions AI tools as mediators that facilitate interactive, visually rich dialogue between youth, urban planners, designers, and policymakers.

The research aims to address a critical gap: while youth and marginalized communities are increasingly recognized as essential stakeholders in urban development, their participation in decision-making processes remains limited. At the same time, Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how urban spaces are imagined and designed. This project seeks to combine these two dynamics in a meaningful and ethical way.

Innovative Objectives of the AI and Climate-Responsive Urban Design PhD Project

The research will investigate several key dimensions, including:

• How Generative AI reshapes participatory practices and power dynamics in urban planning

• The role of cultural, institutional, and governance contexts in adopting AI-mediated design approaches

• How youth engage with AI tools to influence climate-responsive urban futures

By combining theoretical insights with empirical research, the project aims to develop practical frameworks that can be applied in real-world urban planning contexts.

Innovative Responsibilities of the PhD Candidate

The selected candidate will undertake a range of academic and practical responsibilities, culminating in a PhD thesis composed of four high-quality journal articles.

Key responsibilities include:

• Conducting a systematic literature review on AI in participatory planning

• Carrying out qualitative and ethnographic fieldwork in urban environments

• Engaging with policymakers, practitioners, AI developers, and community stakeholders

• Designing and facilitating co-design workshops involving youth and AI tools

• Analyzing ethical, cultural, and institutional dimensions of AI integration

• Disseminating research findings through publications and conferences

• Contributing to teaching activities (approximately 15% of time)

The project adopts a multi-method research approach, including interviews, focus groups, participant observation, and visual-based methodologies.

Innovative Academic Environment at the University of Groningen

The University of Groningen is one of Europe’s leading research institutions, known for its interdisciplinary approach and strong commitment to addressing global challenges. The Faculty of Spatial Sciences plays a critical role in advancing research on sustainability, urban development, and societal transformation.

With a vibrant academic community of over 100 PhD students and international researchers, the faculty provides an environment that fosters innovation, collaboration, and impactful research. The university emphasizes inclusivity, diversity, and a safe working environment, ensuring that all researchers can thrive and contribute meaningfully.

Innovative Benefits and Funding Package

This PhD position offers a competitive and comprehensive funding package in line with Dutch university standards.

Key benefits include:

• Monthly salary ranging from €3,059 to €3,881

• Full-time employment for four years (subject to performance evaluation after the first year)

• 232 vacation hours annually (based on a 38-hour workweek)

• Additional holiday allowance (8%) and end-of-year bonus (8.3%)

• Opportunities for professional and personal development

• Support for research dissemination and international engagement

The position provides not only financial stability but also a strong platform for academic and professional growth.

Innovative Candidate Profile

The program seeks highly motivated and intellectually curious individuals with a strong academic background and interest in interdisciplinary research.

Ideal candidates should have:

• A Master’s degree in urban planning, human geography, urban studies, anthropology, or related fields

• Experience with qualitative research methods such as ethnography, interviews, or focus groups

• Strong communication skills and cultural sensitivity

• Excellent command of English

Additional experience in participatory design, digital tools, or AI ethics is considered an advantage, though advanced technical AI skills are not required.

Deadline for Applications

The deadline to apply for this Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 is 30 April 2026 (23:59 Dutch local time).

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 in AI and Urban Design and apply, click here:

APPLY HERE

Conclusion

The Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 at the University of Groningen offers a rare chance to contribute to transformative research at the intersection of technology, urban planning, and social inclusion. By exploring how Generative AI can empower youth participation in climate-responsive cities, this program positions candidates at the forefront of future urban innovation.

For more scholarship opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here

The post Innovative PhD Opportunity 2026 in AI and Climate-Responsive Urban Design at University of Groningen appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Google PhD Fellowship Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Outstanding Doctoral Researchers
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Google PhD Fellowship Program is now open for applications, offering exceptional doctoral students the opportunity to receive funding, mentorship, and global recognition for their research. This prestigious fellowship is designed to support PhD students conducting innovative research in computer science and related fields, helping them advance their academic careers while connecting with world-class experts. […]

The post Google PhD Fellowship Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Outstanding Doctoral Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Google PhD Fellowship Program is now open for applications, offering exceptional doctoral students the opportunity to receive funding, mentorship, and global recognition for their research.

This prestigious fellowship is designed to support PhD students conducting innovative research in computer science and related fields, helping them advance their academic careers while connecting with world-class experts.

About the Google PhD Fellowship Program

The Google PhD Fellowship recognizes outstanding graduate students who are making significant contributions in research areas aligned with Google’s focus.

Beyond financial support, the fellowship provides access to mentorship from leading researchers, creating a strong bridge between academia and industry.

What the Fellowship Offers

Selected fellows benefit from a robust support package, including:

Full or partial funding covering tuition and living expenses
Mentorship from a Google Research Mentor
Global recognition as a Google PhD Fellow
Access to a network of top researchers and professionals
Opportunities for career growth and collaboration

Important Application Note

Applications cannot be submitted directly by students.

All applications must be submitted through an official university representative during the application window. This means interested candidates should coordinate with their department or institution early to ensure timely submission.

Why You Should Apply

The Google PhD Fellowship is one of the most competitive and prestigious fellowships globally. It not only provides financial relief but also opens doors to collaboration, visibility, and future career opportunities in both academia and industry.

How to Apply

Learn more and start your application process here.

For more Fellowship Opportunites, Visit OFY Here.

The post Google PhD Fellowship Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Outstanding Doctoral Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 for Women-Led Projects Worldwide The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 provide a valuable opportunity for women-led grassroots organizations to access funding and support for impactful, community-driven initiatives. Offered by the American Women for International Understanding (AWIU), these micro-grants aim to strengthen women’s empowerment efforts across low- and middle-income countries by supporting practical, time-bound projects that deliver measurable results. With applications open until April 16, 2026, this grant program is designed to fund initiatives that focus on improving education, health, and economic opportunities for women. Grants range between $1,000 and $10,000, with preference given to projects requesting up to $3,000. Empowering Women Through Grassroots Funding For nearly five decades, AWIU has been committed to advancing the lives of women globally. Initially focused on supporting individual women’s educational opportunities, the organization has since evolved to prioritize funding grassroots initiatives that directly benefit women and their communities. The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 reflect this mission by supporting local organizations that understand the unique challenges faced by women in their regions. By investing in small, targeted projects, AWIU aims to create meaningful and sustainable change at the community level. Empowering Focus Areas for 2026 Grants The AWIU micro-grant program supports projects that fall within three key areas of women’s empowerment: Empowering Education and Skill-Building Projects that provide access to education, vocational training, and skill development opportunities for women and girls. These initiatives aim to enhance knowledge, improve employability, and promote long-term personal and professional growth. Empowering Health and Wellbeing Initiatives that address women’s physical and mental health, including access to healthcare services, awareness programs, and community-based wellness interventions. Empowering Entrepreneurship and Income Generation Projects that support women in building sustainable livelihoods through entrepreneurship, business development, and income-generating activities. These initiatives help women achieve financial independence and contribute to economic development. Empowering Grant Structure and Funding Details The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 are designed to support small-scale, high-impact projects that can be completed within a defined timeframe. Key funding details include: • Grant amounts ranging from $1,000 to $10,000 • Preference for projects requesting up to $3,000 • Project duration of up to 12 months • Funding for specific, clearly defined activities The program emphasizes efficiency and impact, ensuring that funded projects deliver tangible results within a short period. Empowering Project Requirements To be considered for funding, projects must meet several important criteria: • Be led by women or organizations focused on women’s empowerment • Be concrete and clearly defined in scope • Be time-limited (maximum of 12 months) • Demonstrate measurable and quantifiable outcomes • Show potential for broader impact or scalability • Be supported by two verifiable references These requirements ensure that funded initiatives are both impactful and accountable. Empowering Impact Through Measurable Results AWIU places a strong emphasis on measurable outcomes and sustainability. Successful projects are expected to demonstrate clear benefits for women and their communities, whether through improved access to education, better health outcomes, or increased economic opportunities. Additionally, projects that have a multiplier effect—meaning they can create broader, long-term benefits beyond the initial scope—are particularly encouraged. Empowering Application Process and Timeline The application process for the Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 is competitive and time-sensitive. Applications are accepted within a defined window and may close early if the maximum number of submissions is reached. Key Dates: • Application Opens: March 16, 2026 • Application Deadline: April 16, 2026 (or earlier if 200 applications are received) Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to ensure their submissions are considered. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 and apply, click here: https://awiu.org/grant-application/ Conclusion The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 represent a powerful opportunity for women-led organizations to access funding and bring impactful ideas to life. By supporting grassroots initiatives that focus on education, health, and economic empowerment, AWIU continues to drive meaningful change for women around the world. For more grant opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 provide a valuable opportunity for women-led grassroots organizations to access funding and support for impactful, community-driven initiatives. Offered by the American Women for International Understanding (AWIU), these micro-grants aim to strengthen women’s empowerment efforts across low- and middle-income countries by supporting practical, time-bound projects that deliver measurable results. With […]

Offered by the American Women for International Understanding (AWIU), these micro-grants aim to strengthen women’s empowerment efforts across low- and middle-income countries by supporting practical, time-bound projects that deliver measurable results. With applications open until April 16, 2026, this grant program is designed to fund initiatives that focus on improving education, health, and economic opportunities for women. Grants range between $1,000 and $10,000, with preference given to projects requesting up to $3,000. Empowering Women Through Grassroots Funding For nearly five decades, AWIU has been committed to advancing the lives of women globally. Initially focused on supporting individual women’s educational opportunities, the organization has since evolved to prioritize funding grassroots initiatives that directly benefit women and their communities. The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 reflect this mission by supporting local organizations that understand the unique challenges faced by women in their regions. By investing in small, targeted projects, AWIU aims to create meaningful and sustainable change at the community level. Empowering Focus Areas for 2026 Grants The AWIU micro-grant program supports projects that fall within three key areas of women’s empowerment: Empowering Education and Skill-Building Projects that provide access to education, vocational training, and skill development opportunities for women and girls. These initiatives aim to enhance knowledge, improve employability, and promote long-term personal and professional growth. Empowering Health and Wellbeing Initiatives that address women’s physical and mental health, including access to healthcare services, awareness programs, and community-based wellness interventions. Empowering Entrepreneurship and Income Generation Projects that support women in building sustainable livelihoods through entrepreneurship, business development, and income-generating activities. These initiatives help women achieve financial independence and contribute to economic development. Empowering Grant Structure and Funding Details The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 are designed to support small-scale, high-impact projects that can be completed within a defined timeframe. Key funding details include: • Grant amounts ranging from $1,000 to $10,000 • Preference for projects requesting up to $3,000 • Project duration of up to 12 months • Funding for specific, clearly defined activities The program emphasizes efficiency and impact, ensuring that funded projects deliver tangible results within a short period. Empowering Project Requirements To be considered for funding, projects must meet several important criteria: • Be led by women or organizations focused on women’s empowerment • Be concrete and clearly defined in scope • Be time-limited (maximum of 12 months) • Demonstrate measurable and quantifiable outcomes • Show potential for broader impact or scalability • Be supported by two verifiable references These requirements ensure that funded initiatives are both impactful and accountable. Empowering Impact Through Measurable Results AWIU places a strong emphasis on measurable outcomes and sustainability. Successful projects are expected to demonstrate clear benefits for women and their communities, whether through improved access to education, better health outcomes, or increased economic opportunities. Additionally, projects that have a multiplier effect—meaning they can create broader, long-term benefits beyond the initial scope—are particularly encouraged. Empowering Application Process and Timeline The application process for the Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 is competitive and time-sensitive. Applications are accepted within a defined window and may close early if the maximum number of submissions is reached. Key Dates: • Application Opens: March 16, 2026 • Application Deadline: April 16, 2026 (or earlier if 200 applications are received) Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to ensure their submissions are considered. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 and apply, click here: https://awiu.org/grant-application/ Conclusion The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 represent a powerful opportunity for women-led organizations to access funding and bring impactful ideas to life. By supporting grassroots initiatives that focus on education, health, and economic empowerment, AWIU continues to drive meaningful change for women around the world. For more grant opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 provide a valuable opportunity for women-led grassroots organizations to access funding and support for impactful, community-driven initiatives. Offered by the American Women for International Understanding (AWIU), these micro-grants aim to strengthen women’s empowerment efforts across low- and middle-income countries by supporting practical, time-bound projects that deliver measurable results.

With applications open until April 16, 2026, this grant program is designed to fund initiatives that focus on improving education, health, and economic opportunities for women. Grants range between $1,000 and $10,000, with preference given to projects requesting up to $3,000.

Empowering Women Through Grassroots Funding

For nearly five decades, AWIU has been committed to advancing the lives of women globally. Initially focused on supporting individual women’s educational opportunities, the organization has since evolved to prioritize funding grassroots initiatives that directly benefit women and their communities.

The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 reflect this mission by supporting local organizations that understand the unique challenges faced by women in their regions. By investing in small, targeted projects, AWIU aims to create meaningful and sustainable change at the community level.

Empowering Focus Areas for 2026 Grants

The AWIU micro-grant program supports projects that fall within three key areas of women’s empowerment:

Empowering Education and Skill-Building

Projects that provide access to education, vocational training, and skill development opportunities for women and girls. These initiatives aim to enhance knowledge, improve employability, and promote long-term personal and professional growth.

Empowering Health and Wellbeing

Initiatives that address women’s physical and mental health, including access to healthcare services, awareness programs, and community-based wellness interventions.

Empowering Entrepreneurship and Income Generation

Projects that support women in building sustainable livelihoods through entrepreneurship, business development, and income-generating activities. These initiatives help women achieve financial independence and contribute to economic development.

Empowering Grant Structure and Funding Details

The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 are designed to support small-scale, high-impact projects that can be completed within a defined timeframe.

Key funding details include:

• Grant amounts ranging from $1,000 to $10,000

• Preference for projects requesting up to $3,000

• Project duration of up to 12 months

• Funding for specific, clearly defined activities

The program emphasizes efficiency and impact, ensuring that funded projects deliver tangible results within a short period.

Empowering Project Requirements

To be considered for funding, projects must meet several important criteria:

• Be led by women or organizations focused on women’s empowerment

• Be concrete and clearly defined in scope

• Be time-limited (maximum of 12 months)

• Demonstrate measurable and quantifiable outcomes

• Show potential for broader impact or scalability

• Be supported by two verifiable references

These requirements ensure that funded initiatives are both impactful and accountable.

Empowering Impact Through Measurable Results

AWIU places a strong emphasis on measurable outcomes and sustainability. Successful projects are expected to demonstrate clear benefits for women and their communities, whether through improved access to education, better health outcomes, or increased economic opportunities.

Additionally, projects that have a multiplier effect—meaning they can create broader, long-term benefits beyond the initial scope—are particularly encouraged.

Empowering Application Process and Timeline

The application process for the Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 is competitive and time-sensitive. Applications are accepted within a defined window and may close early if the maximum number of submissions is reached.

Key Dates:

• Application Opens: March 16, 2026

• Application Deadline: April 16, 2026 (or earlier if 200 applications are received)

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to ensure their submissions are considered.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 , click  here

Apply here

Conclusion

The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 represent a powerful opportunity for women-led organizations to access funding and bring impactful ideas to life. By supporting grassroots initiatives that focus on education, health, and economic empowerment, AWIU continues to drive meaningful change for women around the world.

 

For more grant opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here

The post Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 for Women-Led Projects Worldwide The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 provide a valuable opportunity for women-led grassroots organizations to access funding and support for impactful, community-driven initiatives. Offered by the American Women for International Understanding (AWIU), these micro-grants aim to strengthen women’s empowerment efforts across low- and middle-income countries by supporting practical, time-bound projects that deliver measurable results. With applications open until April 16, 2026, this grant program is designed to fund initiatives that focus on improving education, health, and economic opportunities for women. Grants range between $1,000 and $10,000, with preference given to projects requesting up to $3,000. Empowering Women Through Grassroots Funding For nearly five decades, AWIU has been committed to advancing the lives of women globally. Initially focused on supporting individual women’s educational opportunities, the organization has since evolved to prioritize funding grassroots initiatives that directly benefit women and their communities. The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 reflect this mission by supporting local organizations that understand the unique challenges faced by women in their regions. By investing in small, targeted projects, AWIU aims to create meaningful and sustainable change at the community level. Empowering Focus Areas for 2026 Grants The AWIU micro-grant program supports projects that fall within three key areas of women’s empowerment: Empowering Education and Skill-Building Projects that provide access to education, vocational training, and skill development opportunities for women and girls. These initiatives aim to enhance knowledge, improve employability, and promote long-term personal and professional growth. Empowering Health and Wellbeing Initiatives that address women’s physical and mental health, including access to healthcare services, awareness programs, and community-based wellness interventions. Empowering Entrepreneurship and Income Generation Projects that support women in building sustainable livelihoods through entrepreneurship, business development, and income-generating activities. These initiatives help women achieve financial independence and contribute to economic development. Empowering Grant Structure and Funding Details The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 are designed to support small-scale, high-impact projects that can be completed within a defined timeframe. Key funding details include: • Grant amounts ranging from $1,000 to $10,000 • Preference for projects requesting up to $3,000 • Project duration of up to 12 months • Funding for specific, clearly defined activities The program emphasizes efficiency and impact, ensuring that funded projects deliver tangible results within a short period. Empowering Project Requirements To be considered for funding, projects must meet several important criteria: • Be led by women or organizations focused on women’s empowerment • Be concrete and clearly defined in scope • Be time-limited (maximum of 12 months) • Demonstrate measurable and quantifiable outcomes • Show potential for broader impact or scalability • Be supported by two verifiable references These requirements ensure that funded initiatives are both impactful and accountable. Empowering Impact Through Measurable Results AWIU places a strong emphasis on measurable outcomes and sustainability. Successful projects are expected to demonstrate clear benefits for women and their communities, whether through improved access to education, better health outcomes, or increased economic opportunities. Additionally, projects that have a multiplier effect—meaning they can create broader, long-term benefits beyond the initial scope—are particularly encouraged. Empowering Application Process and Timeline The application process for the Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 is competitive and time-sensitive. Applications are accepted within a defined window and may close early if the maximum number of submissions is reached. Key Dates: • Application Opens: March 16, 2026 • Application Deadline: April 16, 2026 (or earlier if 200 applications are received) Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to ensure their submissions are considered. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 and apply, click here: https://awiu.org/grant-application/ Conclusion The Empowering AWIU Global Micro-Grants 2026 represent a powerful opportunity for women-led organizations to access funding and bring impactful ideas to life. By supporting grassroots initiatives that focus on education, health, and economic empowerment, AWIU continues to drive meaningful change for women around the world. For more grant opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Transformative Pahara Fellowship 2027 for Education Leaders Driving System Change
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Transformative Pahara Fellowship 2027 presents a powerful and highly selective leadership development opportunity for education leaders committed to reshaping the role of education in society. Designed to bring together a diverse coalition of changemakers, this fellowship focuses on deep reflection, meaningful dialogue, and collaborative leadership across differences. With applications now open and the […]

The post Transformative Pahara Fellowship 2027 for Education Leaders Driving System Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Transformative Pahara Fellowship 2027 presents a powerful and highly selective leadership development opportunity for education leaders committed to reshaping the role of education in society. Designed to bring together a diverse coalition of changemakers, this fellowship focuses on deep reflection, meaningful dialogue, and collaborative leadership across differences.

With applications now open and the deadline set for July 10, 2026, the Pahara Fellowship invites forward-thinking leaders from across the education ecosystem to join a community dedicated to impact, growth, and long-term systemic change.

Transformative Leadership Experience for Education Changemakers

The Pahara Fellowship is not a traditional training program—it is a deeply immersive leadership experience designed to challenge perspectives, build self-awareness, and foster collaboration among leaders with diverse viewpoints. The program seeks individuals who are actively working to reimagine education and are ready to engage in thoughtful dialogue across ideological, cultural, and professional differences.

Through the Transformative Pahara Fellowship 2027, participants will explore the broader role of education in society while strengthening their leadership capacity to drive meaningful change in their communities and beyond.

Transformative Fellowship Structure and Learning Model

The fellowship spans 12 months and is centered around a series of three intensive, week-long in-person seminars held at the Pahara campus in Bailey, Colorado. These seminars are complemented by a virtual orientation and ongoing engagement throughout the year.

The structure includes:

• One virtual orientation session prior to the first seminar

• Three in-person leadership seminars

• Pre-reading and preparation for each session

• Continuous reflection and dialogue across the cohort

Each seminar is designed to create a space for deep personal reflection, intellectual exploration, and authentic engagement with peers.

Transformative Learning Through Dialogue and Reflection

A defining feature of the Pahara Fellowship is its emphasis on text-based dialogue. Participants engage with a curated mix of classical and contemporary readings, many of which extend beyond the field of education. This interdisciplinary approach encourages leaders to think critically about broader societal issues and their connection to education.

The fellowship also integrates elements such as:

• Grounding in nature

• Mind and body connection practices

• Structured dialogue across diverse perspectives

This combination creates a uniquely reflective environment where participants can challenge assumptions, explore new ideas, and grow both personally and professionally.

Transformative Community and Collaboration

The Pahara Fellowship places a strong emphasis on building a diverse and inclusive community of leaders. Cohorts are intentionally designed to reflect a wide range of perspectives, including political ideologies, professional backgrounds, and lived experiences.

Participants are expected to:

• Commit to personal growth and continuous learning

• Engage respectfully with differing viewpoints

• Build meaningful relationships across lines of difference

• Embrace complexity and uncertainty with openness

This approach ensures that the fellowship experience is not only intellectually enriching but also deeply relational and transformative.

Transformative Focus on Education Leadership

The fellowship seeks leaders from across the education sector, including:

• Charter school leaders

• Nonprofit leaders supporting education

• Policy influencers and system leaders

• Innovators working to reshape education systems

There is a particular emphasis on identifying leaders who bring diverse perspectives, including those from underrepresented communities and across the political spectrum.

Transformative Seminar Schedule for 2027 Cohorts

Selected fellows will participate in one of two cohort options, both beginning with a virtual orientation in December 2026.

Option 1:

• Virtual Orientation: December 4, 2026

• Seminar 1: February 1–5, 2027

• Seminar 2: July 12–16, 2027

• Seminar 3: December 6–10, 2027

Option 2:

• Virtual Orientation: December 4, 2026

• Seminar 1: February 22–26, 2027

• Seminar 2: July 19–23, 2027

• Seminar 3: December 13–17, 2027

All seminars are held in person at the Pahara campus in Bailey, Colorado, and full participation is required.

Transformative Application Process

The application process is designed to provide a holistic understanding of each candidate’s leadership journey, motivations, and potential for impact.

Applicants will be required to submit:

• Information about their background, work, and leadership experience

• Responses outlining why the fellowship is timely for them

• Contact details for three references

• A professional bio and resume

The process typically takes approximately 90 minutes to complete, and applicants are encouraged to prepare their responses thoughtfully.

Deadline for Applications

The deadline to apply for the Transformative Pahara Fellowship 2027 is July 10, 2026.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Transformative Pahara Fellowship 2027 and apply, click here:

 

Conclusion

The Transformative Pahara Fellowship 2027 is more than a leadership program—it is a space for reflection, connection, and growth among leaders committed to reshaping education systems. By bringing together diverse perspectives and fostering deep dialogue, the fellowship empowers participants to lead with greater clarity, purpose, and impact.

For more fellowship opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here

The post Transformative Pahara Fellowship 2027 for Education Leaders Driving System Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 for Early-Career Journalists in Criminal Justice
item.dc:creator: Michelle Zyongwe
item.description:
The Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for early-career journalists to strengthen their reporting skills in criminal justice while receiving mentorship, training, and financial support. Organized by the Law & Justice Journalism Project (LJJP), this one-year fellowship is designed to support journalists working in or reporting on Pennsylvania, United States. With applications closing […]

The post Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 for Early-Career Journalists in Criminal Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for early-career journalists to strengthen their reporting skills in criminal justice while receiving mentorship, training, and financial support. Organized by the Law & Justice Journalism Project (LJJP), this one-year fellowship is designed to support journalists working in or reporting on Pennsylvania, United States.

With applications closing on March 26, 2026 (11:59 PM EST), this fellowship is ideal for reporters, editors, producers, and freelancers looking to deepen their expertise in law, crime, and public safety reporting.

Impactful Fellowship to Strengthen Criminal Justice Reporting

The LJJP Fellowship was created to improve the quality of journalism in areas related to crime, policing, courts, and public safety. In an era where misinformation and incomplete narratives can shape public perception, the program emphasizes the importance of accurate, evidence-based, fair, and sensitive reporting.

The Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 equips journalists with the tools and support needed to produce high-quality stories that reflect the complexities of the criminal justice system while maintaining ethical and professional standards.

Impactful Learning Through Mentorship, Training, and Networking

The fellowship is structured around three core pillars that ensure a well-rounded professional development experience:

Impactful Mentorship

Each fellow is paired with an experienced journalist who provides personalized, one-on-one guidance. Mentors support fellows in refining story ideas, improving reporting techniques, accessing records, and strengthening sourcing strategies. This mentorship is designed to enhance the fellow’s existing work rather than create additional workload.

Impactful Training Opportunities

Fellows will participate in regular workshops held monthly or quarterly. These sessions cover key topics such as:

• Data-driven journalism

• Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) requests

• Narrative storytelling

• Investigative techniques

These trainings are tailored to the needs of the fellows, ensuring practical and relevant learning outcomes.

Impactful Networking Connections

Participants gain access to a network of journalists and subject matter experts. This creates opportunities for collaboration, knowledge exchange, and long-term professional relationships within the field of criminal justice reporting.

Impactful Fellowship Structure and Time Commitment

The fellowship runs from April 20, 2026, to April 20, 2027, providing a full year of support and development.

Unlike many intensive programs, the LJJP Fellowship is designed to complement your existing work. Fellows are expected to commit approximately 4–6 hours per month, making it highly flexible and accessible for working professionals.

Importantly, fellows are not required to produce content specifically for LJJP. Instead, they continue working on their existing assignments, whether for an employer or freelance clients. Selected work may be republished through partner platforms such as NC Local.

Impactful Financial Support

Selected fellows will receive a total stipend of $5,000, paid quarterly throughout the fellowship period. This financial support is intended to supplement the fellow’s current income while allowing them to focus on improving their reporting skills and producing impactful journalism.

Who Should Apply

The Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 is open to early-stage journalists who demonstrate a strong interest in criminal justice reporting.

Ideal candidates include:

• Reporters, editors, photographers, TV/radio journalists, and producers

• Freelance journalists

• Individuals with 1–5 years of experience in journalism

• Professionals working in or reporting on Pennsylvania

While prior experience in criminal justice reporting is beneficial, it is not required. What matters most is a genuine interest in the field and a commitment to ethical, high-quality journalism.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following materials:

• Cover letter

• Resume

• Three samples of published work

Applications must be submitted before the deadline, and incomplete submissions will not be considered.

Deadline for Applications

The deadline to apply for the Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 is March 26, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST.

Learn More About This Opportunity

To learn more about the Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 and apply, click here

 

Conclusion

The Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 is a powerful opportunity for early-career journalists to elevate their reporting, gain expert mentorship, and contribute to more accurate and responsible storytelling in the criminal justice space. With flexible commitments, financial support, and access to a strong professional network, this fellowship provides a meaningful pathway for growth and impact.

For more fellowship opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here

The post Impactful LJJP Fellowship 2026 for Early-Career Journalists in Criminal Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now:Erasmus+ Traineeships 2026 at Women Political Leaders (WPL): Internships in Brussels or Remote for Students
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
If you are a university student searching for Erasmus+ traineeships in Europe in 2026, the opportunities at Women Political Leaders (WPL) offer a highly competitive pathway to gain international experience in politics, advocacy, and leadership. These 6-month Erasmus+ internships—available in Brussels, Belgium or remotely—are designed to equip students with practical skills in project management, data […]

The post Apply Now:Erasmus+ Traineeships 2026 at Women Political Leaders (WPL): Internships in Brussels or Remote for Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a university student searching for Erasmus+ traineeships in Europe in 2026, the opportunities at Women Political Leaders (WPL) offer a highly competitive pathway to gain international experience in politics, advocacy, and leadership. These 6-month Erasmus+ internships—available in Brussels, Belgium or remotely—are designed to equip students with practical skills in project management, data analysis, partnerships, and communications while contributing to global efforts to advance women in political leadership.

About Women Political Leaders (WPL)

Women Political Leaders is a global network dedicated to increasing both the number and influence of women in political leadership positions worldwide. Its community includes Presidents, Prime Ministers, Cabinet Ministers, Members of Parliament, and Mayors. Through high-level convenings, research, partnerships, and advocacy, WPL plays a critical role in shaping inclusive governance and policy conversations globally.

Overview of the Erasmus+ Traineeship Programme

All roles are offered under the Erasmus+ Programme, meaning applicants must secure Erasmus+ mobility funding through their home university. These traineeships are:

  • Duration: 6 months
  • Location: Brussels, Belgium or Remote
  • Eligibility: Must be enrolled in a university programme and eligible for Erasmus+ funding
  • Commitment: Preference for candidates available full-time

Each position provides structured mentorship, professional development, and exposure to global political leadership.

1. Project Management Assistant (Erasmus+ Traineeship)

Role Overview

As a Project Management Office Assistant, you will support the planning and execution of high-level initiatives, working closely with global stakeholders and ensuring smooth coordination across multiple projects.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in project planning and tracking deliverables
  • Ensure timelines and milestones are met
  • Support coordination of high-level events and initiatives
  • Prepare reports, dashboards, and documentation
  • Engage stakeholders for effective collaboration
  • Contribute to operational efficiency improvements

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Background in Project Management, Business Administration, Political Science, International Relations, or related fields
  • Strong organisational and time management skills
  • Ability to multitask in a fast-paced, multicultural environment
  • Excellent English communication skills (additional languages are an advantage)
  • Familiarity with Canva, Google Suite, and basic website management

2. Data Analyst Assistant / Junior Data Analyst Officer

Role Overview

This role focuses on leveraging data to support WPL’s mission. You will contribute to impactful research and reporting that influences policymakers globally.

Key Responsibilities

  • Analyse and interpret data for WPL initiatives
  • Develop reports, presentations, and data visualisations
  • Support research on women’s political leadership
  • Collaborate on data-driven strategies
  • Assist with database updates

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Background in Data Science, Statistics, Political Science, or related fields
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Experience with tools like Excel, Python, R, or Power BI
  • Familiarity with Salesforce and Google Suite
  • Interest in global advocacy and public affairs

3. Partnerships Assistant (Erasmus+ Traineeship)

Role Overview

As a Partnerships Assistant, you will play a key role in building and maintaining relationships with stakeholders while supporting funding and collaboration initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

  • Coordinate and support stakeholder meetings
  • Take minutes and track decisions and action points
  • Conduct partner mapping and engagement support
  • Identify funding and grant opportunities
  • Assist with grant documentation and reporting
  • Maintain accurate project and partnership records

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Background in Business Administration, Political Science, International Relations, or related fields
  • Strong organisational and communication skills
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks efficiently
  • Proficiency in Microsoft and Google Suites
  • Familiarity with CRM and project management tools is an advantage

4. Communications Assistant / Junior Communications Officer

Role Overview

This role offers full-cycle exposure to communications in an international advocacy environment, including content creation, design, and storytelling.

Key Responsibilities

  • Create content for social media, websites, and newsletters
  • Draft and edit high-quality, institutionally appropriate copy
  • Design digital assets and presentations
  • Support video editing and storytelling formats
  • Assist with communications for events and advocacy initiatives
  • Manage editorial workflows and content calendars
  • Maintain press databases and handle communications emails

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Background in Communications, Marketing, Journalism, Political Science, or related fields
  • Strong writing and editing skills
  • Experience with Canva and digital storytelling tools
  • Basic website management knowledge
  • Attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines
  • Excellent English proficiency (additional languages are an asset)

What WPL Offers Trainees

Across all roles, trainees benefit from:

  • A dynamic and international working environment
  • Hands-on experience in their respective fields
  • Close mentorship with regular feedback
  • Networking opportunities with global political leaders and experts
  • A traineeship completion certificate
  • A professional reference for strong performance
  • Potential consideration for future opportunities within WPL

Important Eligibility and Selection Notes

  • Applicants must be eligible for the Erasmus+ Programme and secure funding through their university
  • Full-time availability is strongly preferred
  • These roles are highly competitive due to their global exposure and impact

How to Apply

To apply, candidates must submit:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Motivation Letter

Applications should be sent via email to: recruitment@wpleaders.org

Email Subject Lines (Important)

  • Project Management: Erasmus+ Project Management Internship – [Your Name]
  • Data Analyst: Erasmus+ Data Analyst Internship – [Your Name]
  • Partnerships: Erasmus+ Partnerships Internship – [Your Name]
  • Communications: Erasmus+ Communications Internship – [Your Name]

These Erasmus+ traineeships at Women Political Leaders provide a rare opportunity to gain real-world experience at the intersection of politics, advocacy, and international collaboration. Whether your interest lies in project management, data analysis, partnerships, or communications, this programme equips you with the skills, exposure, and network needed to thrive in global leadership and policy environments.

For students passionate about shaping inclusive political systems and advancing women’s leadership worldwide, this traineeship is more than just an internship—it is a launchpad for impactful careers.

Discover more similar opportunities here.

The post Apply Now:Erasmus+ Traineeships 2026 at Women Political Leaders (WPL): Internships in Brussels or Remote for Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NATO Internship Programme 2027 (Paid): 6-Month Internship in Brussels for Students & Graduates
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Key Details Programme: NATO Internship Programme 2027 Location: Brussels Duration: 6 months Start Dates: March or September 2027 Deadline: 30 April 2026 Funding: Paid (Approx. EUR 1,335/month) Eligibility: Students (3rd year+) or recent graduates Application Mode: Online (TALEO system) If you would like to gain experience working for a political and military alliance that promotes […]

The post NATO Internship Programme 2027 (Paid): 6-Month Internship in Brussels for Students & Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Details

  • Programme: NATO Internship Programme 2027
  • Location: Brussels
  • Duration: 6 months
  • Start Dates: March or September 2027
  • Deadline: 30 April 2026
  • Funding: Paid (Approx. EUR 1,335/month)
  • Eligibility: Students (3rd year+) or recent graduates
  • Application Mode: Online (TALEO system)

If you would like to gain experience working for a political and military alliance that promotes freedom and security , then the NATO Internship Programme might be for you!

NATO offers six-month internships to applicants in the third year of their university studies, or recent graduates of a bachelor or master’s programme. Internships apply to a broad range of expertise and skillsets, providing opportunities in areas like political affairs, defence and security, innovation, operations, communications, business support and human resources, finance, science and technology, and infrastructure and facilities.

Internships take place at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium – an exciting city that serves as a meeting point for many European and other international organisations, and a hub of activity and networking for young professionals.

The NATO Internship Programme offers a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to learn from the NATO community while gaining six months of valuable work experience and a greater knowledge of the inner workings of the Alliance!

Main objectives of the NATO Internship Programme

  1. To provide interns with the opportunity to learn from the NATO community and get a better understanding and a more balanced view of the Organization.
  2. To improve NATO’s access to theoretical and technical knowledge that the intern can apply through practical work assignments.
  3. To contribute to creating a more diverse workforce.
  4. To expand knowledge and understanding of NATO in member countries.

The NATO Internship Programme offers internship positions within the NATO International Staff, the International Military Staff and some of the NATO agencies and bodies at NATO Headquarters.

 

What we offer

  • Internship stipend:
    If you are selected for the NATO-funded internship programme, you will receive a monthly stipend (EUR 1,335 in 2026). This amount changes by year in line with inflation and NATO salary adjustments.
  • Travel:
    Once the Internship Office has confirmed your start date, you will be provided with a prepaid flight/train ticket from your place of origin at a return economy rate, and for an amount of up to EUR 1,200. If you are traveling to Brussels by car, these costs will be reimbursed.
  • Leave:
    You will be entitled to 15 days of paid leave during the six-month internship period.
  • In-house training:
    During the internship, you will take part in an induction programme and monthly briefings to provide you with an overview of NATO’s activities and structure. The briefings present unique opportunities to meet experienced staff and officials from across the Alliance, to discuss topics of interest with them and with other interns. Visits to other institutions, like the European Parliament, will also be organised.

NATO is committed to diversity and inclusion and strives to provide equal access to career opportunities regardless of gender, age, nationality, ethnic origin, religious belief, cultural background, sexual orientation or disability. NATO welcomes applicants from all member countries and strongly encourages women to apply.

As an employer, NATO values integrity, transparency and accountability in accordance with international norms and practices established for the defence and related security sector.

 

Eligibility criteria

To apply for a NATO internship, you must fulfil the following criteria on the closing date for applications:

  • Age: 21+
  • Nationality: a citizen of a NATO member country
  • Educational status: at least two years of university completed and enrolled in a third year OR having obtained your highest degree less than a year prior
  • Languages: proficiency in one of the two official NATO languages: English or French. A working knowledge of the second is considered an asset. Knowledge of other languages may also be considered an asset for certain positions.

If you are a national of a NATO partner country, you can contact your mission at NATO Headquarters or embassy in Brussels directly for information about potential internships within the mission or embassy.

NATO Member Countries (Eligibility Requirement)

Applicants must hold citizenship in one of the following NATO member countries:

  • Albania
  • Belgium
  • Bulgaria
  • Canada
  • Croatia
  • Czech Republic
  • Denmark
  • Estonia
  • Finland
  • France
  • Germany
  • Greece
  • Hungary
  • Iceland
  • Italy
  • Latvia
  • Lithuania
  • Luxembourg
  • Montenegro
  • Netherlands
  • North Macedonia
  • Norway
  • Poland
  • Portugal
  • Romania
  • Slovakia
  • Slovenia
  • Spain
  • Sweden
  • Türkiye
  • United Kingdom
  • United States

If you are from a NATO partner country, you may explore internship opportunities through your national mission or embassy in Brussels.

Application process

We invite you to use the e-recruitment system, TALEO, to submit your application. We are unable to accept spontaneous applications, or applications sent by mail or email.

To apply for a NATO internship, you must submit your application during the open call, which begins around April or May every year.

We encourage you to apply well in advance of the deadline! It may take us up to three working days to address any requests for support (including for technical issues). Therefore, if you contact us on the final day of the application period, we may not be able to respond before the portal is closed.

You do not have to fill in the entire application form in a single session. You can save your information as a draft and log in later to amend and/or finalise it until the closing date. Applications will not be accepted after the closing date.

In your application, please provide as many relevant details as possible for the position you are interested in. We advise you to read the requirements of each internship position carefully, and to apply only if you meet those requirements. You may apply to a maximum of three positions.

The selection process

What happens once you have submitted your application through NATO’s e-recruitment portal?

  1. Application review

The screening and evaluation of applications are conducted in the month following the deadline. You will receive an invitation to complete an online assessment on a platform called HireVue. The assessment will consist of a series of interactive business games, as well as a recorded video interview. Once you receive the invitation, you will have two weeks to complete the online assessments. Your application will also be reviewed by the Internship Programme Office to ensure that you meet the required qualifications of the position(s) for which you applied before being referred to hiring managers. If your application is not successful at this stage, you will be informed.

  1. Selection

Shortlisted candidates will be contacted around June or July and invited for an interview. Final selections will be made sometime between July and September. Successful candidates will be invited to begin their internships in March or September of the following year. If selected, the start date of your internship will be determined based on the needs of the NATO body and your availability.

  1. Offer and security clearance

If selected for an internship, you will receive a conditional offer letter via your TALEO account stating the period of the internship, the internship placement you have been selected for, and documents you must complete in order for NATO to launch your security clearance procedure. Unsuccessful candidates will be informed.

The security clearance procedure varies from one country to another, but takes on average six to eight months to complete. You will be responsible for following your progress via your national delegation to NATO. Contact information for your delegation will be provided in your conditional offer letter. You must gain a security clearance prior to the start of your internship. In the event that the security clearance procedure takes longer than expected, it may be possible to postpone the start date of your internship.

Additional requirements before your start date

Upon the start of your internship, you must prove that you have obtained health insurance for the duration of your internship. You will be required to fill out a request form for a special identity card and submit it to NATO’s Privileges and Immunities Office, which will liaise with the Belgian authorities. This special identity card will serve as a residence permit during your stay in Belgium. You are exempt from this procedure if you hold Belgian citizenship.

Employment after completion of your internship

Internship placements do not guarantee further employment by NATO. In order to gain employment by NATO at any point following your internship, you must participate in the standard recruitment process.

 

Timeline

Due to a high number of applications, dates may vary slightly each year.

Online application opens

April – May

Preselection

June – July

Final selection

July – September

On-boarding period

Main intakes take place in March and September of the year following the application, depending on the security clearance procedure. For example, if your application in April 2026 is successful, your starting date will be 1 March or 1 September 2027. The security clearance procedure varies from one country to another, but takes on average six to eight months to complete.

Resources

  • NATO Internship Factsheet (PDF/640KB)
  • NATO Internship Programme – Guide for Newcomers (PDF/6.5MB)

Internship programmes in other NATO bodies

  • SHAPE Internship Programme
  • NSPA Internships
  • Programme de stages de l’ACT
  • HQ LC Internship Programme (Izmir)opens 
  • NCI Agency Internship Programme
  • HQ AIRCOM Internship Programme
  • STO Early-Career Scientists Programme

 

Learn more about NATO internships and Apply Now

FAQs : Frequently asked questions about NATO’s internship programme

The post NATO Internship Programme 2027 (Paid): 6-Month Internship in Brussels for Students & Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Grants for African Women Scientists
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for women researchers across Africa to gain international recognition, funding, and professional development support. For over 25 years, the Fondation L’Oréal and UNESCO have collaborated to empower women in science globally. In 2026, this prestigious initiative […]

The post L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Grants for African Women Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for women researchers across Africa to gain international recognition, funding, and professional development support.

For over 25 years, the Fondation L’Oréal and UNESCO have collaborated to empower women in science globally. In 2026, this prestigious initiative takes a bold step forward by merging the South African National Programme and the Sub-Saharan Africa Regional Programme into a single, unified Pan-African platform. This transformation creates a powerful, borderless network of scientific excellence across all 49 Sub-Saharan African countries.

This program is designed to identify, support, and elevate the next generation of African women scientists who are driving innovation and shaping the future of global research.

Program Overview

The 2026 edition of the programme seeks outstanding women researchers from leading laboratories and research institutions across Sub-Saharan Africa. It aims to strengthen scientific leadership on the continent while promoting gender equality in science.

Participants will become part of a highly respected international community of researchers and gain access to funding, training, and visibility on a global stage.

What the Programme Offers

Selected candidates will benefit from a combination of financial support and professional development opportunities, including:

1. Research Grants

  • 30 doctoral candidates will receive €10,000 each
  • 8 post-doctoral researchers will receive €15,000 each

These grants are designed to support research advancement, fieldwork, equipment, and academic development.

2. Capacity Building & Training

Participants will receive specialized training in:

  • Leadership development
  • Research management
  • Negotiation skills
  • Scientific communication

3. International Recognition

  • Join a prestigious network of award-winning scientists
  • Gain visibility within the global scientific community
  • Enhance academic and professional credibility

Eligible Research Fields

Applicants must be conducting research in one of the following disciplines:

  • Formal Sciences
  • Life Sciences
  • Environmental Sciences
  • Physical Sciences
  • Engineering
  • Technological Sciences

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for this programme, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Nationality & Location

  • Must be a citizen of a Sub-Saharan African country (including South Africa)
  • Must be conducting research within a recognized institution in Sub-Saharan Africa

For Doctoral Candidates

  • Must be currently enrolled in a PhD program
  • Must be in at least the second year of study (first-year students are not eligible)

For Post-Doctoral Researchers

  • Must already be engaged in postdoctoral research
  • Must have started research before the call opened
  • Must have defended their PhD thesis after February 2021
  • The eligibility window is extended by one year per child (maternity consideration)

General Condition

  • Previous recipients of any L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science awards are not eligible

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Scientific excellence and innovation of the research project
  • Overall quality and clarity of the application
  • Ability to communicate scientific ideas effectively
  • Potential to contribute to scientific advancement and societal impact

Application Timeline

  • Call Opens: March 11, 2026
  • Application Deadline: May 15, 2026
  • Final Selection Announcement: July 6, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare and submit their applications well before the deadline.

Why You Should Apply

This programme is more than just a research grant—it is a gateway to global recognition and career advancement. By applying, you will:

  • Gain access to funding that supports impactful research
  • Strengthen your leadership and professional skills
  • Join a Pan-African network of leading women scientists
  • Position yourself as a change-maker in your field
  • Contribute to solving some of Africa’s most pressing challenges

How to Apply

To apply for the L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026:

  • Ensure you meet all eligibility requirements
  • Prepare a strong research proposal and supporting documents
  • Submit your application through the official platform

Click HERE to Apply

Learn more about the L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026 HERE.

Visit OFY HERE to discover more global Award opportunities, and click HERE for more global Grant opportunities.

The post L’Oréal-UNESCO For Women in Science Sub-Saharan Africa Programme 2026: Fully Funded Research Grants for African Women Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Reality Leadership Training 2026 in Nashville, USA: Fully Funded Global Climate Leadership Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are passionate about climate action, sustainability, environmental advocacy, and global leadership, then the The Climate Reality Project Nashville Training 2026 is one of the most impactful international opportunities currently open for applications. This globally recognized program equips individuals with the knowledge, tools, and network needed to become influential climate leaders in their communities […]

The post Climate Reality Leadership Training 2026 in Nashville, USA: Fully Funded Global Climate Leadership Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about climate action, sustainability, environmental advocacy, and global leadership, then the The Climate Reality Project Nashville Training 2026 is one of the most impactful international opportunities currently open for applications. This globally recognized program equips individuals with the knowledge, tools, and network needed to become influential climate leaders in their communities and beyond.

What Is the Climate Reality Leadership Corps Training?

The Climate Reality Leadership Corps training is a high-impact global initiative designed to train individuals to become effective climate advocates and communicators.

Participants will:

  • Train directly with former U.S. Vice President Al Gore and leading climate experts
  • Gain a deep understanding of the climate crisis and practical solutions
  • Learn advocacy, communication, and organizing skills
  • Join a global network of over tens of thousands of climate leaders across more than 190 countries

The program has trained thousands of individuals worldwide to lead climate action efforts at local, national, and global levels.

Why You Should Apply: Key Benefits of the Program

1. World-Class Training and Mentorship – Participants receive direct training from globally recognized experts, including Al Gore, who has been a leading voice in climate advocacy for decades.

2. Global Networking Opportunities – You will join a diverse network of climate leaders, activists, policymakers, and professionals from across continents, enabling long-term collaborations.

3. Certification as a Climate Reality Leader – Upon completion, you become an official Climate Reality Leader, opening doors to global advocacy platforms and initiatives.

4. Access to Tools and Resources – Participants gain access to:

  • Advocacy toolkits
  • Communication materials
  • Ongoing training and mentorship programs
  • Global campaigns and community platforms

5. Career and Impact Growth – This opportunity is ideal for building a career in:

  • Climate policy
  • Environmental advocacy
  • Sustainable development
  • International development

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

The program is highly inclusive and designed for global participation.

Eligible Applicants Include:

  • Students (high school, undergraduate, postgraduate)
  • Young professionals
  • Activists and community leaders
  • Entrepreneurs and innovators
  • Policy advocates
  • Educators and researchers

Age Requirements:

  •  Minimum age:
    • 13 years (U.S. applicants)
    • 16 years (international applicants)

Nationality Requirements:

  • Open to applicants worldwide
  • Individuals from Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, and Oceania are eligible

The program is intentionally global, encouraging diverse perspectives in climate action.

Required Qualifications

Unlike many competitive international programs, this training does not require strict academic qualifications. However, strong applicants typically demonstrate:

  • Passion for climate action and sustainability
  • Interest in advocacy, leadership, or community engagement
  • Commitment to implementing climate solutions locally
  • Ability to communicate and influence change

No specific degree, GPA, or professional experience is required—making it accessible to a wide range of applicants.

What Does the Training Cover?

The Nashville training is an intensive, immersive experience focused on real-world climate leadership.

Key Learning Areas:

  • Climate science and global impacts
  • Renewable energy and clean solutions
  • Climate justice and equity
  • Public speaking and communication strategies
  • Grassroots organizing and mobilization
  • Policy advocacy and political engagement

Participants will also learn how to:

  • Deliver impactful climate presentations
  • Organize campaigns
  • Influence decision-makers
  • Build community-based climate initiatives

Financial Information: Is It Fully Funded?

The program is free to attend, but it is not fully funded.

What Is Covered:

  • Training participation
  • Some meals during the event

What You Must Cover:

  • International or local travel
  • Accommodation
  • Additional meals

However, scholarship opportunities may be available, and selected applicants may receive financial assistance depending on availability.

Application Process

To apply, candidates must:

  1. Complete the online application form via the official website
  2. Provide personal and professional details
  3. Demonstrate motivation and interest in climate leadership
  4. Submit before the deadline

Selection Process:

  • Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis
  • Selected candidates receive an email confirmation
  • Final participation requires confirmation and logistics planning

Apply here

Why This Opportunity Matters in 2026

The 2026 Nashville training is particularly significant because:

  • It marks 20 years of climate leadership training by the organization
  • It brings together global leaders at a critical time for climate action
  • It aims to expand the global network of climate advocates

With climate change becoming one of the most urgent global challenges, this training empowers individuals to drive real change.

Deadline for Application

  • Deadline: Not strictly fixed (Rolling applications)
  • Recommendation: Apply as early as possible before slots are filled

Since applications are reviewed continuously, early applicants have a higher chance of selection.

The The Climate Reality Project Nashville Training 2026 is not just a conference—it is a gateway to becoming a global climate leader, joining a powerful international network, and gaining the skills needed to influence real-world change.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Climate Reality Leadership Training 2026 in Nashville, USA: Fully Funded Global Climate Leadership Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Advocacy Coalition Internship 2026: A Fully Funded Mental Health Advocacy Opportunity for Young Leaders Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The Youth Advocacy Coalition (YAC) Internship Program by The Jed Foundation is one of the most impactful global opportunities for young people passionate about mental health, policy advocacy, and youth leadership. Designed to empower the next generation of changemakers, this program equips participants with practical advocacy skills, real-world policy exposure, and a platform to influence […]

The post Youth Advocacy Coalition Internship 2026: A Fully Funded Mental Health Advocacy Opportunity for Young Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Youth Advocacy Coalition (YAC) Internship Program by The Jed Foundation is one of the most impactful global opportunities for young people passionate about mental health, policy advocacy, and youth leadership. Designed to empower the next generation of changemakers, this program equips participants with practical advocacy skills, real-world policy exposure, and a platform to influence decision-makers.

If you are a student or young leader looking to make a difference in mental health systems, this internship could be your gateway into national and global advocacy.

About the Youth Advocacy Coalition (YAC)

The Youth Advocacy Coalition was created to amplify youth voices in mental health policy and reform. It brings together passionate young advocates and connects them with policymakers, organizations, and institutions working to improve mental health systems.

The initiative was launched in collaboration with advocacy partners to ensure that young people play a central role in shaping policies that directly affect their lives.

Participants are selected based on their impact in their communities and commitment to mental health advocacy, making this a highly competitive and prestigious opportunity.

What the Internship Offers

1. Paid Internship Experience

The YAC internship is a paid opportunity, ensuring that participants are financially supported while contributing to meaningful advocacy work.

2. High-Level Advocacy Training

Interns undergo structured training in:

  • Mental health policy and systems
  • Advocacy strategies
  • Public storytelling and media engagement
  • Civic participation and leadership

3. Mentorship from Experts

Participants receive guidance from:

  • Policy experts
  • Mental health professionals
  • Advocacy leaders

4. Real Policy Engagement

Interns engage directly with:

  • Government officials
  • Advocacy organizations
  • Policy stakeholders

They also participate in Advocacy Days on Capitol Hill, gaining firsthand experience in influencing public policy.

5. National Exposure

Interns present their work at major events such as:

  • JED’s Annual Policy Summit
  • National advocacy forums

This exposure helps build credibility and opens doors to future opportunities.

Program Structure and Activities

The internship follows a hands-on, project-based learning model, including:

  • Weekly training sessions on advocacy and policy
  • Development of a strategic mental health advocacy project
  • Collaboration with fellow youth leaders
  • Engagement in awareness campaigns and storytelling

Participants also continue their advocacy work beyond the internship, extending impact throughout the academic year.

Who Can Apply (Eligibility Criteria)

Target Category of Applicants

This opportunity is designed for:

  • Undergraduate students
  • Young leaders and youth advocates
  • Individuals passionate about mental health and policy change

Key Qualifications

Applicants are typically expected to:

  • Demonstrate strong interest or experience in mental health advocacy
  • Show leadership and community impact
  • Have the ability to collaborate and communicate effectively
  • Be committed to driving social change

Participants in the broader coalition are often selected through a rigorous vetting process based on their advocacy impact and leadership.

Eligible Countries and Regions

  • Primarily open to students in the United States (core program focus)
  • However, international students studying in U.S. institutions may also be eligible
  • Some virtual components and advocacy engagement may allow global participation depending on cohort structure

Because program specifics may evolve annually, applicants from:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Europe
  • Latin America

are encouraged to monitor updates, especially if enrolled in international or U.S.-affiliated institutions.

Why You Should Apply

1. Build a Career in Policy and Advocacy – This internship provides a direct pathway into policy-making and public health advocacy careers.

2. Gain Real-World Impact – Participants contribute to actual policy discussions and reforms, not just theoretical learning.

3. Join a Powerful Network – You will connect with:

  • Youth leaders
  • Policymakers
  • Advocacy organizations

4. Address a Global Crisis – With rising rates of depression and anxiety among young people, initiatives like YAC are critical in shaping mental health solutions.

Application Process (General Guide)

While exact steps may vary yearly, applicants typically:

  1. Complete an online application form
  2. Submit personal and academic details
  3. Provide evidence of advocacy or leadership experience
  4. Possibly attend interviews or screening sessions

Selection is highly competitive, so demonstrating real impact and passion is essential.

Apply here

Deadline

10th April 2026

The Youth Advocacy Coalition Internship by is more than just an internship—it is a transformational leadership experience. It empowers young people to step into decision-making spaces and actively shape the future of mental health policy.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Youth Advocacy Coalition Internship 2026: A Fully Funded Mental Health Advocacy Opportunity for Young Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
